method_atom: A field-theoretic construction on a space with one extra continuous coordinate in which the geometric/metric structure is generalized from a fixed-scale (length-preserving) one to a scale-covariant connection where scale can vary along paths, yielding a self-consistent smooth solution that spontaneously produces localized, spatially separated wall-like profiles along the extra coordinate. Different modes of the fundamental field become trapped on different walls, so the exponentially small overlap between two separated bound modes converts a mere spatial separation into an exponentially large ratio of effective coupling strengths. The spectrum of small fluctuations around the solution is discrete (quantized) and free of unstable (negative-norm/imaginary-frequency) modes.
object_atom: Five-dimensional Kaluza-Klein gravity: a spacetime with one effectively compact extra dimension respecting Z2 symmetry, containing thick smooth gravitational branes, with the massless graviton localized on the brane at the origin and matter degrees of freedom confined to a second brane, addressing the Planck/electroweak mass-hierarchy problem a la Randall-Sundrum with a tachyon-free quantized Kaluza-Klein mass spectrum.
brief_reason: The extra-dimension plus scale-covariant-geometry machinery that self-generates separated localized walls and turns exponentially small mode overlaps into a hierarchy of scales is transferable, while the graviton, matter branes, warped spacetime, and the gravitational mass-hierarchy problem are the domain-specific object.
